reforms.description: Mexico aims to enhance its startup ecosystem by promoting high-tech industrial clusters that establish linkages with universities and early-stage investment vehicles, alongside introducing targeted policy tools to support innovative firms and the creation of the National Institute of the Entrepreneur, which implements schemes to address entrepreneurs' needs, including a seed capital 'fund of funds' for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to foster innovation and economic growth by supporting startups and enhancing linkages with research institutions.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Institute of the Entrepreneur was established in 2013 and has been operational since.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly enhance the support structure for startups but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: A National Institute of the Entrepreneur was created in 2013 to refine and implement schemes to address entrepreneurs’ needs, including forming a seed capital 'fund of funds'.

reforms.description: The new National Productivity Commission has been established to improve coordination and identify policy reforms, showing early signs of effective operation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the commission is expected to enhance policy coordination, which can lead to improved productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The commission has been announced and is in the early stages of operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the commission represents a significant step towards better policy coordination but is not a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: As a central part of the Programme to Democratise Productivity, the new National Productivity Commission has been established to improve coordination and identify policy reforms.

reforms.description: In late 2012, Mexico introduced a comprehensive labour reform law aimed at stimulating the creation of formal employment through innovative measures such as short-term training contracts and limitations on back-pay during litigation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is designed to enhance the labour market, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The labour reform law was enacted and has been in effect since late 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the labour market aimed at fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The comprehensive labour reform law of late 2012 contained initiatives intended to stimulate the creation of formal employment.

reforms.description: In 2014, Mexico's National Council for Science and Technology (CONACYT) increased its funding by 20%, focusing on transversal innovation policies to enhance development in high-impact industry sectors and improve cooperation with universities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in funding is aimed at enhancing innovation capabilities and collaboration, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increase was executed in 2014 and has been in effect since.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding increase is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: The funding available to the National Council for Science and Technology (CONACYT) was increased by 20% in 2014.

reforms.description: The Aerospace Research and Innovation Network of Querétaro (RIIAQ) was established in 2008 to strengthen research, technological development, and innovation capabilities in the aerospace sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the network is intended to enhance collaboration and innovation in the aerospace sector, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The network was established in 2008 and has been operational since.
reforms.importance_rationale: The network represents a significant initiative to enhance innovation in a specific sector but is not a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Key to Querétaro’s success has been the close relationship between the state government and private industry. An important co-ordination mechanism between the industry, education and research institutions is the Aerospace Research and Innovation Network of Querétaro (RIIAQ).
